title: Circuit de Barcelona-Catalunya
sub: F1’s definitive aero benchmark and every team’s winter-testing home. A flowing, high-energy lap that gobbles front tyres and exposes any weakness in a car.

sectors.name: Sector 1
sectors.char: Uphill braking into Turn 1, the Turn 2-3 right (huge lateral load), and the Turn 4 left.

sectors.name: Sector 2
sectors.char: The long Turn 5 right, the slow Turn 7-8, and the fast Turn 9. Where tyre wear is decided.

sectors.name: Sector 3
sectors.char: Heavy braking at La Caixa (T10), the slow section, then the fast final corners.

tyreAlloc: Hardest in the range · C1 / C2 / C3
stratNote: High lateral energy through the long Turn 3 and Turn 9 makes this one of the toughest on tyres. Pirelli brings its hardest compounds; degradation drives the call.
spots: End of the main straight + DRS, uphill heavy braking. The main move.
Heaviest braking on the lap after a DRS zone; a strong secondary spot.
Occasional lunge into the long right on a good exit from Turn 4.
otNote: Notoriously hard to pass: everyone knows the track and dirty air through the long corners hurts. The 2026 Straight Mode zones aim to open it up.
corners: Uphill heavy braking off the main straight + DRS. Primary overtaking spot.
Tightens into the long Turn 3; commitment on entry sets the lap.
Long, loaded right that piles energy into the front-left.
Quick left dropping toward the middle sector.
Big, long right-hander; another sustained-load corner.
Blind, fast right over a crest.
Slow left opening the technical part of sector 2.
Short right linking the slow complex.
Fast, demanding right loading the tyres before the final sector.
Heaviest braking on the lap (~310 to ~106 km/h) + DRS. A real chance.
Slow right in the stop-and-go final complex.
Quick left building toward the fast finish.
Fast right that flows into the last corner.
Final right launching onto the main straight and DRS 1.
geo: Used for winter testing for 30+ years, with a corner of every type. If a car works here it works everywhere.
The long Turn 3 and Turn 9 pile sustained lateral energy into the front-left. Tyre management often decides the race.
An exposed site where wind swings the balance lap to lap. A stable platform through the fast final sector is key.
The FIA mapped four Straight Mode zones for 2026, adding attack points to fight the old overtaking problem.
